title: Kitchen Compost Guide
values: Create a frictionless compost drop zone
Choose a small lidded container and keep it where prep scraps already happen so composting becomes the default path.
Add fruit peels, coffee grounds, tea leaves, eggshells, and vegetable scraps. Keep the list visible near the station.
Keep meat, dairy, oily leftovers, and look-alike plastic bags out of the compost bin so the pickup stays clean.
Empty the bin before moisture builds up
Transfer the countertop scraps to the outdoor compost cart on the same day each week so the station never becomes a cleanup chore.
Reset the station for the next cycle
Quickly rinse the small bin, let it dry, and drop in a fresh liner or newspaper layer before returning it to the counter.
Make the process self-serve for housemates
Tape a short card above the station that shows what is compostable and when the curb cart gets emptied.
